expectations.text: has_all_sections: Spec has all required sections
expectations.evidence: All required headings are present in `SPEC.md`: `## Problem` (line 12), `## Solution` (line 18), `## Scope` (line 24), `## Design Decisions` (line 43), `### Failure Modes` (line 61), `## Risks` via `## Risks & Open Questions` (line 68), `## Alternatives` via `## Alternatives Considered` (line 77), and `## Verification` (line 84).

expectations.text: no_code_fences: No code fences outside Implementation Notes
expectations.evidence: No triple-backtick code fences (```) appear anywhere in `SPEC.md:1-115`, including outside the implementation section.

expectations.text: no_ts_interfaces: No TypeScript types in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: `SPEC.md:45-60` contains prose-only decision statements; no TypeScript interface/type syntax is present.

expectations.text: no_proto_definitions: No proto/interface definitions for the template type
expectations.evidence: The spec references a new proto/API in prose (`SPEC.md:20`, `SPEC.md:29`) but does not include any proto/interface block definitions.

expectations.text: no_exact_file_paths_in_scope: Scope is behavioral, not file-based
expectations.evidence: In-scope bullets (`SPEC.md:28-34`) are behavior/capability statements and contain no file paths; scope content is overwhelmingly behavioral (>80%).

expectations.text: spec_under_120_lines: Under 120 lines excluding Implementation Notes
expectations.evidence: `SPEC.md` has 115 total lines (`SPEC.md:1-115`), which is below the 120-line limit.

expectations.text: bold_topic_sentences: Bold topic sentences in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: All 8/8 Design Decisions paragraphs open with bold lead decision statements (`SPEC.md:45`, `47`, `49`, `51`, `53`, `55`, `57`, `59`) = 100%.

expectations.text: implementer_can_start: Implementer could start without questions
expectations.evidence: Core implementation decisions are covered: template model/API ownership (`SPEC.md:20`, `28-30`, `45`), variable fill UX and validation (`SPEC.md:30`, `32-34`, `49`, `53`), both entry points (`SPEC.md:30-32`, `49`), panel-query prefill behavior (`SPEC.md:22`, `31`, `51`, `64`), and template management constraints (`SPEC.md:38`, `40`).

expectations.text: no_implementation_leaks: Design Decisions are decisions, not implementation
expectations.evidence: `SPEC.md:45-60` has no code fences, TS types, JSON shapes, mechanism tables, exact line references, hook-level implementation details, or exact new file paths.

Both entry points are explicitly designed: Alerts picker (`SPEC.md:30`) and Dashboard panel context action (`SPEC.md:31`), with shared flow/API convergence (`SPEC.md:49`) and query-derived prefill policy (`SPEC.md:22`, `51`, `64`).

expectations.text: failure_modes_are_policy: Failure modes are policy decisions
expectations.evidence: Failure modes are policy-level behaviors in 4/4 cases (`SPEC.md:63-66`): retry/fallback policy, partial-prefill + explicit completion policy, server validation + preserve inputs policy, and submit-time conflict review policy.

claims.claim: The spec designs both creation entry points (Alerts page and Dashboard panel context menu).
claims.evidence: Explicitly specified in `SPEC.md:30-32` and reinforced by shared-flow decision in `SPEC.md:49`.

claims.claim: Dashboard-triggered creation pre-fills template variables from the panel query but remains user-confirmed.
Templates are server-managed with shared API/proto contracts rather than client constants.

claims.evidence: `SPEC.md:45-60` is prose-only with policy/design intent and no forbidden implementation artifacts.

suggestions.reason: This is structurally useful but weakly discriminating: a spec with empty or one-line sections would still pass. Add minimum-content/decision-quality checks per section.
Line-count alone can reward terse-but-underspecified specs. Pair it with a density or coverage assertion tied to required decision areas to avoid false positives.

eval_feedback.overall: Current eval set is generally strong on anti-leakage and cross-module coverage, with minor gaps in discriminating section substance versus mere structure/length.
